name: Abdulwadoud
ongoingProjectsSummary:
Building and contributing to entrepreneurship ecosystems at the intersection of startups, research, and innovation.

As Community & Ecosystem Manager at Campus Foundery OWL, I support early-stage founders, organize ecosystem initiatives, and connect university-driven innovation with regional stakeholders.

In parallel, I co-founded Aram Lab, a research-driven think tank focused on mapping and developing the Syrian startup ecosystem, bridging academia and real-world entrepreneurial impact.

Previously co-founded a climate-focused packaging startup developing EPS-free sustainable materials.

name: Adriana
ongoingProjectsSummary:
Currently finishing my Data Science degree at IE University while working as a Data Analyst, where I build data pipelines and analytics infrastructure.

Alongside that, I lead IE's Technology & Innovation Club (7,000+ members) organizing large-scale tech events, hackathons and industry conferences, most recently TechIE 2026. I'm also part of IE's MXT Program, an exponential mindset program at the intersection of management and technology, where I engage weekly with founders and operators building globally.

Super interested in Product, and anything that involves tech, people and data. I also like to do my research on biotech, bioinformatics and neuroscience.

name: Adrien
ongoingProjectsSummary: I work at the intersection of robotics, autonomous systems, AI, and HCI. I’m currently a student researcher in HCI, and I’m moving toward AI-focused work next. In parallel, I work at Microsoft on security and AI, and I also have an entrepreneurial background, having built and contributed to projects with multi-million scale impact.

name: Alessandro
ongoingProjectsSummary:
I am currently working on four parallel tracks. First, my startup Refinea, a GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) platform currently in closed beta, backed by Bocconi's B4i pre-acceleration program and supported by $125K in cloud credits from Microsoft and Google. Second, I lead theHackLab, a student association I co-founded at Bocconi that organizes startup, finance, and data science competitions for 400+ students each semester. Third, I am leveraging the proprietary dataset collected through Refinea's monitoring pipeline to write my bachelor thesis on quantifying the measurable impact of GEO practices across different industries. Fourth, at the National University of Singapore I am completing coursework projects in network analysis of global trade flows and optimization of music recommendation systems.

On a more personal note, I am working on visiting as many corners of the planet as I can, currently at 35 countries across all continents.

name: Amin
ongoingProjectsSummary: Welinor is an AI-native financial concierge that acts as a personal family office for high-income individuals with complex financial lives, aggregating accounts, files, and context across banks, brokers, crypto, and businesses to optimize decisions and manage complexity. We connect users with best-in-class service providers and handle coordination, tax prep, and advisory underwriting for the affluent segment that traditional family offices can't economically serve below €10-50M in assets.

name: Andreea Elena
ongoingProjectsSummary: I am currently working on research and engineering projects at the intersection of AI, natural language processing, and privacy. I focus on identifying and mitigating privacy risks in retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems and agents. I developed an open-source framework and application for privacy-preserving RAG document processing. In parallel, I am building AI-powered applications using agentic workflows and LLMs, with experience in developing production-ready systems for public sector use cases and legal tech.

name: Ansh

name: Anthony Gerardo
ongoingProjectsSummary: Currently in the Entrepreneur First London Spring 2026 batch, searching for a technical co-founder and validating startup theses in BCI/neurotech (on-device EEG denoising), defense/dual-use AI, and customs compliance automation. Exiting HandyGo, a WhatsApp-first ERP for field service SMEs I built and piloted with 3 clients (40% ops time saved). Finishing my Computer Engineering degree at Polimi with active projects in multi-agent AI for elderly care and VHDL digital logic design.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

name: Antonino Emanuele
ongoingProjectsSummary:
I am working on shifting artificial intelligence from software simulations into physical realities. Currently, AI does not actually learn; it only simulates the learning process through software on standard processors, resulting in massive energy consumption. My research focuses on unconventional computing algorithms where the physical hardware itself adapts and learns. This approach mirrors biological energy efficiency and raises fundamental questions about the true nature of machine cognition.

In parallel, I am building an early-stage B2B SaaS platform that automates inbound and outbound customer management. The objective of this business is to bootstrap the capital required to pioneer these foundational deep-tech hardware advancements from the ground up, rather than simply developing another iterative AI wrapper.

name: Artur
ongoingProjectsSummary: I'm building a hardware-agnostic software abstraction layer for neuromorphic chips, positioning as the "CUDA moment" for brain-inspired compute. Think developer platform + enterprise licensing for next-generation edge AI hardware. Alongside the startup, I'm completing my thesis about trade-offs in performance and computational efficiency for spiking neural networks

In addition, I recently advanced to the next phase of the med-dev x Wellster Digital Health Hackathon, where we are now developing an early-stage medtech and telemedicine startup idea together with the support of partners such as Wellster Healthtech Group and Spira Labs.

name: Éabha
ongoingProjectsSummary: Currently building a tactile stimulation rig that applies controlled textures to the hand for use with EEG to measure neural responses to texture.

name: Emma S.
ongoingProjectsSummary:
I am building CaptuREE, a deep-tech/clean-tech start-up developing a technology to recover Rare earth elements (REE) from e-waste and other precious sources. Our goal at CaptuREE is to make urban mining a source of REE for supply chain diversification across the world, and create a resilient raw material ecosystem in Europe, without relying on imports from other countries.

In parallel, I am re-building DLAI, a MAKE student association at EPFL aimed at introducing undergraduate students to the world of protein design and academic research. DLAI allows students to build their own protein-design project from zero, learning SOTA methods in the field from computation all the way to wet lab validation and testing. Starting from summer 2026, I will be re-organising the core education poll of the association with the title of President.

I am also building BuildEU, an AI-aided grant search database that will help entrepreneurs find (non)-dilutive funding opportunities across Europe. In a fragmented star-up ecosystem such as ours, I thought helpful to create an all-in-one platform that guides entrepreneurs across the myriad of different opportunities depending on their stage, project field, and location. BuildEU does exactly that, using LLM-based search features to connect founders with the most interesting oportunity for them.

name: Emmanuel
ongoingProjectsSummary: I am building a robotic companion that can support older persons in their homes daily.

name: Eren
ongoingProjectsSummary:
Building Previa Health, using computer vision and AI to decode how the human body moves. We make invisible musculoskeletal patterns visible from any smartphone camera. No hardware, no clinic. Preventive screening accessible to anyone.
Oura built the sleep layer. Whoop built recovery. The movement layer doesn't exist yet.

Beyond Previa: exploring the intersection of personalized oncology and AI, in the Spira Labs incubation track backed by Picus Capital and advising the leadership of a €120M+ German healthcare portfolio on AI strategy.

name: Nicole
ongoingProjectsSummary: I'm completing my Master's thesis at ESPCI Paris on directed evolution of DNA polymerases for next-generation DNA synthesis and molecular data storage, using AlphaFold3-guided structural engineering and high-throughput screening of mutagenic libraries. I'm also building SynthOrder with Nona Research Foundation - a tool to streamline DNA synthesis ordering across providers - and developing AI-powered clinical decision systems for precision oncology using mechanistic pathway simulations.

name: Tord
ongoingProjectsSummary:
I'm the CBOof Shin Starr Presents Inc., the company behind OLHSO, the world's first fully autonomous Korean BBQ restaurant concept. We operate a flagship location in San Mateo generating $4.8M+ in annual revenue, and are currently scaling robotic food trucks and express units into airports (LAX via URW, SFO pipeline) and hotel venues across the Bay Area.

The core mission: build a restaurant that operates 24/7 with minimal staffing, in the footprint of a parking spot, with menus and cuisines updated entirely via software. No queues, no late-night closures, no chef dependency.

Previously I scaled FundingStack/Foundersuite, a fundraising platform that facilitated $10B+ in capital for 20,000+ startups globally.
